message.content:
In the repo at /Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile, find out how developers run services locally. Look for:
1. README files at the root or in docs/
2. Any CONTRIBUTING, DEVELOPMENT, or onboarding docs
3. Skaffold, Tilt, telepresence, or similar local dev tooling config files
4. Any Makefile at the root with run/dev targets
5. Any .env.example or local config examples

Report concisely what the local dev workflow appears to be. Search breadth: medium.

attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll help you find out how developers run services locally in this repo. Let me search for the relevant documentation and configuration files.
Now let me read the root README and Makefile, plus check for docs directory:

content.content:
1	# Mobile Monorepo
2	
3	[![CircleCI](https://circleci.com/gh/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile.svg?style=shield&circle-token=f77e472062f60f7ad1de471939f7d73a00f01757)](https://circleci.com/gh/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile)
4	
5	A repository containing all things mobile. Also see [mobile manifests](https://github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile-manifests).
6	
7	# Table of Contents
8	
9	1. [Makefile](#makefile)
10	1. [Deploying](#deploying)
11	1. [Repository Layout](#repository-layout)
12	1. [Using this Repository](#using-this-repository)
13	    1. [Protocol Buffers](#protocol-buffers)
14	        1. [GRPC Gateways](#grpc-gateways)
15	        1. [REST Clients](#rest-clients)
16	1. [Contributing](#contributing)
17	1. [Documentation](#documentation)
18	
19	## Makefile
20	
21	A [Makefile](./Makefile) exists in the root directory of the monorepo to help perform various tasks. Such recipes include:
22	
23	| command | based on changes* | description |
24	| --- | --- | --- |
25	| `make test` | ✓ |  executes unit tests for only changed packages | 
26	| `make test-all` || executes all unit tests | 
27	| `make sh-fmt` || executes [`sh-fmt`](https://github.com/mvdan/sh) for all shell files |
28	| `make proto-fmt` || formats Protocol Buffer (`protos/*.proto`) files (requires `clang-format` to be installed) |
29	| `make go-fmt` || executes [`gofumpt -s` and `gofumports -local`](https://github.com/mvdan/gofumpt) for non-generated Go code |
30	| `make fmt` || executes all formatters (`*-fmt` Makefile commands) |
31	| `make lint` || performs linting (see [.golangci.yaml](./.golangci.yaml)) |
32	| `make install` || downloads Go dependencies using `go mod download` |
33	| `make buf-gen` || generates Go Protocol Buffer libraries and Swagger definitions |
34	| `make clients` || generates REST clients for all Protocol Buffers with `service` definitions |
35	| `make generate` || executes `go generate ./...` |
36	| `make clean` || cleans up build artifacts |
37	| `make docker-up`|| starts the [docker-compose](./docker/docker-compose.yaml) stack |
38	| `make docker-down`|| stops the [docker-compose](./docker/docker-compose.yaml) stack |
39	| `make install-git-hooks` || installs optional Git hooks |
40	| `make install-git-message` || installs commit message template |
41	| `make check` || runs `make fmt lint test-all`. Useful for checking if CI will pass |
42	| `make circleci-build-changed` | ✓ | uses the circleci tool to execute the `buildChanged` CircleCLI workflow |
43	| `make circleci-build-all` || uses the circleci tool to execute the `buildAll` CircleCLI workflow |
44	| `make build-all-binaries` || builds changed all binaries |
45	| `make build-changed-binaries` | ✓ | builds changed Go binaries |
46	| `make build-container` || builds a single Docker container (takes a `service=my-service` argument) |
47	| `make build-containers` || builds containers for binaries contained in `bin/` |
48	| `make push-containers` || pushes containers for binaries contained in `bin/` |
49	| `make deploy-containers` || deploys containers for binaries contained in `bin/` to dev |
50	| `make containers-all` || compiles all Go binaries and builds all Docker containers |
51	| `make containers` | ✓ | compiles changed Go binaries and builds changed Docker containers |
52	
53	*"based on changes" means that the command operates only on files which have been changed between `HEAD` and `origin/master`.
54	
55	## Deploying
56	
57	To deploy to `<environment>-dev` you will need to create a manifest in the [mobile manifests](https://github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile-manifests)
58	repository. Upon merging your telecom-mobile pull-request CircleCI will automatically create an image tag patch using Kustomize and commit it to the master branch of mobile-manifests. Your change will then be picked up by ArgoCD and deployed. Deploying to production remains a manual task. For more information on deploying services to production see the documentation in the [mobile-manifests](https://github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile-manifests) repository.
59	When deploying a new service don't forget to add it to the [list of services](https://wiki.uw.systems/posts/service-tiers-cqth110e).
60	
61	
62	## Repository Layout
63	
64	* [cmd](cmd) - useful tools related to the monorepo or CI.
65	* [docker](docker) - Docker containers and docker-compose stacks.
66	* [docs](docs) - service documentation. Also contains API specifications.
67	* [githooks](githooks) - contains optional git hooks. Install with `make install-git-hooks`.
68	* [pkg](pkg) - importable mobile Go packages.
69	* [protos](protos) - Protocol Buffer contracts.
70	* [scripts](scripts) - useful scripts, mostly used by CI.
71	* [services](services) - services organised by deployment unit.
72	
73	As a general rule, this repository follows the [Golang standards project layout](https://github.com/golang-standards/project-layout).
74	
75	## Using this Repository
76	
77	### Protocol Buffers
78	
79	All Protocol Buffer contracts are stored in the `protos/` directory. We use a common naming convention to identify Protocol Buffers that belong to a service; for example the `OrderService` can be found under `services/order-service` and its corresponding Protocol Buffers can be found under `protos/order-service.proto`. Using such a naming convention allows for easy identification of ownership.
80	
81	When authoring a new Protocol Buffer ensure that you correctly set the package name and options. This is especially important
82	when authoring a service definition. Below is an example from the `OrderService`:
83	
84	```protobuf
85	syntax = "proto3";
86	package order_service;
87	
88	option go_package = "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/order_service";
89	
90	//...
91	```
92	
93	**Note:** the name of the package (both `package` and `go_package`) use underscores, not hyphens.
94	
95	#### GRPC Gateways
96	
97	If your Protocol Buffer declares a `service` it's likely that you'll want a GRPC gateway deployed - running `make protos` automatically 
98	generates the corresponding `.pb.gw.go` file for you in the `pkg` directory for the service, as well as Swagger 
99	definitions (v2) under `docs/swagger/`. Swagger files are automatically copied into Docker containers under `/app/swagger.json` in line with our standard swagger-ui implementation.
100	
101	**Note:** In order for the Swagger definitions to be correctly copied into Docker containers you must ensure that the package name in the Protocol Buffer file follows our naming convention.
102	
103	#### REST Clients
104	
105	In addition to GRPC Gateways, this repositories tooling can also automatically generate a corresponding REST Go client implementation using the OpenAPI spec. Executing `make clients` automatically builds all Protocol Buffers and generate clients for each service under the `pkg/clients/generated` directory.
106	
107	## Contributing
108	
109	Create a GitHub pull-request, please use the following template for your commit messages;
110	
111	```text
112	# [<tag>] (If applied, this commit will...) <subject> (Max 72 char)
113	# |<----   Preferably using up to 50 chars   --->|<------------------->|
114	# Example:
115	# [feat] Implement automated commit messages
116	
117	# (Optional) Explain why this change is being made
118	# |<----   Try To Limit Each Line to a Maximum Of 72 Characters   ---->|
119	
120	# (Optional) Provide links or keys to any relevant tickets, articles or other resources
121	# Example: Github issue #23
122	
123	# --- COMMIT END ---
124	# Tag can be
125	#    feat     (new feature)
126	#    fix      (bug fix)
127	#    refactor (refactoring code)
128	#    style    (formatting, missing semi colons, etc; no code change)
129	#    doc      (changes to documentation)
130	#    test     (adding or refactoring tests; no production code change)
131	#    version  (version bump/new release; no production code change)
132	#    dbg      (Changes in debugging code/frameworks; no production code change)
133	#    hack     (Temporary fix to make things move forward; please avoid it)
134	#    WIP      (Work In Progress; for intermediate commits to keep patches reasonably sized)
135	#    defaults (changes default options)
136	#
137	# Note: Multiple tags can be combined, e.g. [fix][test] Fix issue X and added test X
138	# --------------------
139	# Remember to:
140	#   * Capitalize the subject line
141	#   * Use the imperative mood in the subject line
142	#   * Do not end the subject line with a period
143	#   * Separate subject from body with a blank line
144	#   * Use the body to explain what and why vs. how
145	#   * Can use multiple lines with "-" or "*" for bullet points in body
146	# --------------------
147	```
148	
149	You can do this automatically by using the `make install-git-message` utility which will present you with the correct
150	template when authoring a commit message
151	
152	**DO NOT PUSH DIRECTLY TO MASTER.** Doing so will result in image tag patches becoming out-of-sync.
153	
154	## Documentation
155	- [Technical diagrams](https://wiki.uw.systems/topics/technical-diagrams-t76b54dy)
156	- [List of services and tiers](https://wiki.uw.systems/posts/service-tiers-cqth110e)
157

1	include .core/Makefile
2	
3	TEAM := mobile
4	BRANCH := main
5	IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX := telecom/mobile
6	MANIFESTS_REPO := git@github.com:utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile-manifests.git
7	LINT_VERSION=v2.11.4
8	
9	FILES = $(shell ls services)
10	
11	buf-gen:
12		docker build --progress=plain \
13			--secret id=gh_auth_token,env=GH_AUTH_TOKEN \
14			--build-arg services="${FILES}" \
15			-t telecom-mobile-protos \
16			-f buf.Dockerfile \
17			--output . \
18			.
19	
20	buf-gen-clean:
21		docker buildx prune
22	
23	start-environment:
24		docker-compose -f ./docker/docker-compose.yaml --profile dev up
25	
26	clean-env:
27		docker compose -f ./docker/docker-compose.yaml --profile dev down
28

content.text: Let me check for .env.example files and look at the docker-compose.yaml:
Let me check the docker-compose.yaml file:

content.content:
1	version: "3.1"
2	
3	services:
4	  zookeeper:
5	    container_name: zookeeper
6	    image: wurstmeister/zookeeper
7	    ports:
8	      - "2181:2181"
9	
10	  kafka:
11	    container_name: kafka
12	    image: wurstmeister/kafka
13	    ports:
14	      - "9092:9092"
15	    environment:
16	      KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT: zookeeper:2181
17	      KAFKA_LISTENERS: "INTERNAL://kafka:9090,EXTERNAL://0.0.0.0:9092"
18	      KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS: "INTERNAL://kafka:9090,EXTERNAL://localhost:9092"
19	      KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP: "INTERNAL:PLAINTEXT,EXTERNAL:PLAINTEXT"
20	      KAFKA_INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME: INTERNAL
21	      AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS: "true"
22	
23	  kafka-ui:
24	    container_name: kafka-ui
25	    image: obsidiandynamics/kafdrop
26	    ports:
27	    - 9000:9000
28	    environment:
29	      KAFKA_BROKERCONNECT: "kafka:9090"
30	      JVM_OPTS: "-Xms32M -Xmx64M"
31	      SERVER_SERVLET_CONTEXTPATH: "/"
32	
33	  mongo:
34	    hostname: mongo
35	    container_name: mongo
36	    image: mongo:4.2.0
37	    ports:
38	      - 27017:27017
39	    entrypoint: [ "/usr/bin/mongod", "--bind_ip_all"]
40	
41	  ui:
42	    container_name: mongo-ui
43	    image: mongo-express:0.49.0
44	    links:
45	      - "mongo"
46	    depends_on:
47	      - "mongo"
48	    ports:
49	      - 9090:8081
50	    environment:
51	      ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_SERVER: mongo
52	
53	  jaeger:
54	    container_name: jaeger
55	    image: jaegertracing/all-in-one:1.19
56	    ports:
57	      - "6831:6831/udp"
58	      - "6832:6832/udp"
59	      - "5778:5778"
60	      - "16686:16686" # HTTP UI
61	      - "14268:14268"
62	      - "14250:14250"
63	      - "9411:9411"
64	    environment:
65	      COLLECTOR_ZIPKIN_HTTP_PORT: "9411"
66

content.text: Let me check if there's a .core/Makefile that might have more local dev targets:

content.content:
1	VERSION := "v2.0.0"
2	
3	TEAM=fixed-line
4	BRANCH?=main
5	DOCKER_ID?=telecom
6	REGISTRY?=registry.uw.systems
7	IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX?=telecom/fixed-line
8	
9	KUSTOMIZE_VERSION?=5.1.0
10	MANIFESTS_REPO?=git@github.com:utilitywarehouse/telecom-fixed-line-manifests.git
11	MANIFESTS_OVERLAY_PATH?=overlays/dev/merit
12	
13	BUILDENV?=
14	BUILDENV += CC=$$(which musl-gcc)
15	BUILDENV += CGO_ENABLED=1
16	BUILDENV += GOARCH=amd64
17	BUILDENV += GOPRIVATE=github.com/utilitywarehouse
18	TESTFLAGS?=-test.v -cover --race -count=1 -failfast -mod=readonly
19	TESTCOVERFLAGS?=-coverprofile=coverage.txt
20	BUILDFLAGS?=-ldflags='-w -s -linkmode external'
21	
22	PB_VERSION=26.1
23	LINT_VERSION=v2.11.4
24	CLANG_FORMAT_VERSION=1:11.0-51+nmu5
25	SHFMT_VERSION=v3.6.0
26	GOIMPORTS_VERSION=v0.5.0
27	GOFUMPT_VERSION=v0.4.0
28	
29	.PHONY: version
30	version:
31		@echo "telecom-monorepo-tools version ${VERSION}"
32	
33	.PHONY: update
34	update:
35		@./.core/scripts/update-tools.sh
36	
37	test:
38		BRANCH=$(BRANCH) TESTFLAGS="$(TESTFLAGS)" $(BUILDENV) ./.core/scripts/tests.sh
39	
40	test-all:
41		$(BUILDENV) go test $(TESTFLAGS) ./...
42	
43	.PHONY: test-cover
44	test-cover:
45		BRANCH=$(BRANCH) TESTFLAGS="$(TESTFLAGS) $(TESTCOVERFLAGS)" $(BUILDENV) ./.core/scripts/tests-cover.sh
46	
47	.PHONY: test-cover-all
48	test-cover-all:
49		TESTFLAGS="$(TESTFLAGS) $(TESTCOVERFLAGS)" $(BUILDENV) ./.core/scripts/tests-cover-all.sh
50	
51	.PHONY: proto-fmt
52	proto-fmt:
53		@./.core/scripts/fmt.sh ${CLANG_FORMAT_VERSION}  ${SHFMT_VERSION} ${GOIMPORTS_VERSION} ${GOFUMPT_VERSION} true
54	
55	.PHONY: fmt
56	fmt:
57		@./.core/scripts/fmt.sh ${CLANG_FORMAT_VERSION}  ${SHFMT_VERSION} ${GOIMPORTS_VERSION} ${GOFUMPT_VERSION}
58	
59	.PHONY: check-format
60	check-format:
61		@./.core/scripts/check-format.sh ${PB_VERSION} ${CLANG_FORMAT_VERSION}  ${SHFMT_VERSION} ${GOIMPORTS_VERSION} ${GOFUMPT_VERSION}
62	
63	.PHONY: install-hooks
64	install-hooks:
65		@cp .core/hooks/pre-commit .git/hooks/pre-commit
66		@chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
67	
68	lint:
69		@./.core/scripts/lint.sh ${LINT_VERSION}
70	
71	.PHONY: install
72	install:
73		$(BUILDENV) go mod download
74	
75	.PHONY: install-tools
76	install-tools: install
77		@cat tools.go | grep _ | awk -F'"' '{print $$2}' | xargs -tI % go install %
78	
79	.PHONY: buf-gen
80	buf-gen:
81		docker build --progress=plain \
82			--secret id=gh_auth_token,env=GH_AUTH_TOKEN \
83			--build-arg services="${FILES}" \
84			-t telecom-mobile-protos \
85			-f buf.Dockerfile \
86			--output . \
87			.
88	
89	.PHONY: buf-gen-clean
90	buf-gen-clean:
91		docker buildx prune
92	
93	.PHONY: clients
94	clients: generate
95		@TEAM=$(TEAM) ./.core/scripts/clients.sh
96	
97	.PHONY: generate
98	generate:
99		@go generate ./...
100	
101	clean:
102		@rm -rf pkg/clients
103	
104	check: fmt lint test-all
105	
106	changed:
107		.core/scripts/changed.sh
108	
109	build-binary:
110		@rm -rf bin && mkdir bin
111		if [ -f  ./services/$$service/main.go ]; then $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/main.go; else $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/cmd/$$service; fi
112	
113	
114	build-all-binaries:
115		@rm -rf bin && mkdir bin
116		for service in $$(ls services); do \
117			echo "compiling $$service" && \
118			if [ -f  ./services/$$service/main.go ]; then $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/main.go; else $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/cmd/$$service; fi; \
119		done
120	
121	build-changed-binaries:
122		@rm -rf bin && mkdir bin
123		export BRANCH=${BRANCH} && for service in $$(.core/scripts/changed.sh); do \
124			echo "compiling $$service" && \
125			if [ -f  ./services/$$service/main.go ]; then $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/main.go; else $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/cmd/$$service; fi; \
126		done
127	
128	build-container: build-binary
129		mkdir -p docker/context/$$service && \
130		cp -r bin/$$service services/$$service/assets docker/context/$$service 2>/dev/null || : && \
131		docker build --build-arg SERVICE=${service} -t ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:latest -f $$(test -f services/$$service/Dockerfile && echo services/$$service/Dockerfile || echo .core/docker/Dockerfile.service) docker/context/$$service && \
132		rm -rf docker/context
133	
134	build-containers:
135		for service in $$(ls bin); do \
136			echo "building $$service" && \
137			mkdir -p docker/context/$$service && \
138			cp -r bin/$$service services/$$service/assets docker/context/$$service 2>/dev/null || : && \
139			docker build --build-arg SERVICE=$$service -t ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:latest -f $$(test -f services/$$service/Dockerfile && echo services/$$service/Dockerfile || echo .core/docker/Dockerfile.service) docker/context/$$service; \
140		done
141		rm -rf docker/context
142	
143	push-containers:
144		docker login -u $(DOCKER_ID) -p ${DOCKER_PASSWORD} ${REGISTRY}
145		for service in $$(ls bin); do \
146			version=$$(git rev-parse HEAD) && \
147			if [ "${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}" != "" ]; then branch=${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}; else branch=${GITHUB_REF_NAME}; fi && \
148			hash=$$(echo ${GITHUB_SHA} | head -c 8) && \
149			branch="$$(echo $$branch | sed 's/[\/_]/-/g')" && \
150			echo "pushing $$service:$$version" && \
151			echo "pushing $$service:$$branch-$$hash" && \
152			docker tag ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:latest ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:$$version && \
153			docker tag ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:latest ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:$$branch-$$hash && \
154			docker push ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:$$version; \
155			docker push ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:$$branch-$$hash; \
156			docker push ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:latest; \
157		done
158	
159	containers-all: build-all-binaries build-containers
160	containers: build-changed-binaries build-containers
161	
162	# The following *-binaries-parallel recipes are used by CI tools to distribute Docker operations
163	# amongst N concurrent workflow jobs. This works by taking the ordinal index of a compiled binary
164	# and performing a modulo operation against the number of available runners. If the modulo outcome
165	# equals the runner index then the operation is performed by the runner.
166	#
167	# For example:
168	# 	If `ls bin | sort` produced `app-1 app-2 app-3 app-4`, then running
169	#	`make ci-build-all-binaries-parallel index=1 runners=2` would result
170	#	in `building app-2` and `building app-4`. This is because 1 (building
171	#	app-2 ordinal index) % 2 (number of runners) == 1 (runner index). The
172	#	same applies to app-4 (ordinal index 3).
173	ci-build-all-binaries-parallel:
174		@rm -rf bin && mkdir bin
175		i=0; \
176		for service in $$(ls services | sort); do \
177			mod=$$(($$i % $(runners))); \
178			if [ $$mod = $(index) ]; then \
179				echo "compiling $$service" && \
180				if [ -f  ./services/$$service/main.go ]; then $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/main.go; else $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/cmd/$$service; fi; \
181			fi; \
182			i=$$(($$i+1)); \
183		done
184	
185	ci-build-changed-binaries-parallel:
186		@rm -rf bin && mkdir bin
187		i=0; \
188		export BRANCH=${BRANCH} && for service in $$(.core/scripts/changed.sh | sort); do \
189			mod=$$(($$i % $(runners))); \
190			if [ $$mod = $(index) ]; then \
191				echo "compiling $$service" && \
192				if [ -f  ./services/$$service/main.go ]; then $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/main.go; else $(BUILDENV) go build $(BUILDFLAGS) -o bin/$$service ./services/$$service/cmd/$$service; fi; \
193			fi; \
194			i=$$(($$i+1)); \
195		done
196	
197	ci-generate-build-artifacts:
198		mkdir -p artifacts
199		version=$$(git rev-parse HEAD) && \
200		for service in $$(ls -p bin | grep -v /); do \
201			echo $$version > artifacts/$$service; \
202		done
203	
204	ci-deploy-containers:
205		git clone $(MANIFESTS_REPO) manifests
206		wget https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases/download/kustomize%2Fv${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}/kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
207	    tar xzvf kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
208	    sudo mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
209	    sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
210	    test -f /usr/local/bin/kustomize
211		bins=$$(ls artifacts) && \
212		cd manifests/${MANIFESTS_OVERLAY_PATH} && \
213		for service in $$bins; do \
214			kustomize edit set image ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service=${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:${GITHUB_SHA}; \
215		done
216		cd manifests && git add ${MANIFESTS_OVERLAY_PATH}/kustomization.yaml && git commit -m "Deploying ${GITHUB_SHA}"
217		cd manifests && git push -u origin ${BRANCH}
218		rm -rf manifests
219	
220	ci-list-images:
221		if [ ! -d artifacts ]; then echo "No artifacts created"; exit 0; fi && \
222		bins=$$(ls artifacts) && \
223		if [ "${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}" != "" ]; then branch=${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}; else branch=${GITHUB_REF_NAME}; fi && \
224		hash=$$(echo ${GITHUB_SHA} | head -c 8) && \
225		for service in $$bins; do \
226	  		echo ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-$$service:$$branch-$$hash; \
227		done
228	
229	ci-raise-manifest-pr-dev:
230		@git clone $(MANIFESTS_REPO) manifests-dev
231		@wget -q https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases/download/kustomize%2Fv${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}/kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
232		tar xzf kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
233		sudo mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
234		sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
235		test -f /usr/local/bin/kustomize
236	
237		@export MANIFEST_PATH=${MANIFESTS_OVERLAY_PATH} && \
238		export REGISTRY=${REGISTRY} && \
239		export IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX=${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX} && \
240		export BRANCH=${BRANCH} && .core/scripts/raise_pr_dev.sh
241	
242	ci-raise-manifest-pr-prod:
243		@git clone $(MANIFESTS_REPO) manifests-prod
244		@wget -q https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases/download/kustomize%2Fv${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}/kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
245		tar xzf kustomize_v${KUSTOMIZE_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz && \
246		sudo mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
247		sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kustomize && \
248		test -f /usr/local/bin/kustomize
249	
250		@export MANIFEST_PATH=${MANIFESTS_OVERLAY_PATH} && \
251		export REGISTRY=${REGISTRY} && \
252		export IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX=${IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX} && \
253		.core/scripts/close_prs_prod.sh && .core/scripts/raise_pr_prod.sh
254

content.text: Let me check the .core/README.md and any DEVELOPMENT files:

content.content:
1	# Telecom Fixed Line Monorepo Tools
2	
3	A repository containing tooling for telecom monorepos. This repository is designed to be a tooling "bolt-on" for monorepos, providing common funcionality shared across projects. Such as Go binary and Docker image compilation, linting, proto generaton, githooks and more.
4	
5	All functions provided by this repository are exposed as Makefile recipes. They include:
6	
7	| command | based on changes* | description |
8	| --- | --- | --- |
9	| `make version` || prints the monorepo tools version |
10	| `make update` || updates the embedded monorepo tools version |
11	| `make test` | ✓ |  executes unit tests for only changed packages | 
12	| `make test-all` || executes all unit tests | 
13	| `make sh-fmt` || executes [`sh-fmt`](https://github.com/mvdan/sh) for all shell files |
14	| `make proto-fmt` || formats Protocol Buffer (`protos/*.proto`) files (requires `clang-format` to be installed) |
15	| `make go-fmt` || executes [`gofumpt -s` and `gofumports -local`](https://github.com/mvdan/gofumpt) for non-generated Go code |
16	| `make fmt` || executes all formatters (`*-fmt` Makefile commands) |
17	| `make lint` || performs linting (see [.golangci.yaml](./.golangci.yaml)) |
18	| `make install` || downloads Go dependencies using `go mod download` |
19	| `make protos` || generates Go Protocol Buffer libraries and Swagger definitions |
20	| `make clients` || generates REST clients for all Protocol Buffers with `service` definitions |
21	| `make generate` || executes `go generate ./...` |
22	| `make clean` || cleans up build artifacts |
23	| `make check` || runs `make fmt lint test-all`. Useful for checking if CI will pass |
24	| `make build-binary service=my-service-name` || builds a single go binary.  |
25	| `make build-all-binaries` || builds all binaries |
26	| `make build-changed-binaries` | ✓ | builds changed Go binaries |
27	| `make build-container service=my-service-name` || builds a single Docker container |
28	| `make build-containers` || builds containers for binaries contained in `bin/` |
29	| `make push-containers` || pushes containers for binaries contained in `bin/` |
30	| `make containers-all` || compiles all Go binaries and builds all Docker containers |
31	| `make containers` | ✓ | compiles changed Go binaries and builds changed Docker containers |
32	
33	Other recipes exist, but are not designed to be executed by the user. Instead, they should be run by a CI tool such as CircleCI or GitHub Actions. The documentation for such recipes can be found in the Makefile.
34	
35	*"based on changes" means that the command operates only on files which have been changed between the branch `HEAD` and `origin/master`.
36	
37	## Installation
38	
39	To install the monorepo tools in your monorepo, clone the repository into `.core` (`git clone git@github.com:utilitywarehouse/telecom-monorepo-tools.git .core`). After you've done this once, you can perform updates using `make update`.
40	
41	### Requirements
42	
43	This repository makes assumptions about your monorepo structure. For full support, your monorepo must:
44	
45	- house your copy of telecom-monorepo-tools in <monorepo-root>/.core
46	- house your service main.go at <monorepo-root>/services/<service-name>/cmd/<service-name>/main.go
47	- house your protocol buffers in <monorepo-root>/protos
48	- provide a `.golangci.yaml` configuration file at <monorepo-root>/.golangci.yaml
49

content.text: Let me check one of the service READMEs to see if they contain local run instructions:

content.content:
1	# Account Gateway
2	
3	## The problem
4	
5	The account identity service provides a new API that is intended to be used to retrieve the latest account information.
6	
7	The Kafka topic it provides has been marked as deprecated, but it is still in use. To prevent fraud, we need to extract
8	certain information from the Account projection, such as the date of the most recent phone number or email update.
9	
10	## Maintainability
11	
12	Account identity data is required by several services within the telecom domain. If the API specification changes, all
13	clients would need to be updated.
14	
15	With the Account Gateway, we can avoid this by providing a single point of access for account information.
16	
17	
18	## Trade-offs
19	
20	The Account Gateway acts as a single point of access for account information. This means that if the gateway fails, all
21	dependent services will be affected.
22	
23	However, it also allows us to centralise the logic for retrieving account information, which can improve maintainability
24	and reduce code duplication.
25	
26	## The solution
27	
28	A projector listens to the Account Identity Kafka topic and persists changes in a MongoDB database, along with the
29	timestamp of the most recent phone number and email updates.
30	
31	The gRPC service, when handling a "Get Account" request, merges the stored data with the response from the Account
32	Identity API.
33

content.text:
Based on my search of the repository, here's what I found about the local development workflow:

## Local Development Workflow Summary

**Primary Setup & Execution:**
1. **Start Environment**: Use `make start-environment` (Makefile target at root) to spin up Docker Compose dependencies
   - This runs: `docker-compose -f ./docker/docker-compose.yaml --profile dev up`
   - Starts: Kafka, Zookeeper, MongoDB, Jaeger (tracing), and Mongo Express UI

2. **Cleanup**: Use `make clean-env` to tear down: `docker compose -f ./docker/docker-compose.yaml --profile dev down`

**Key Makefile Targets for Development:**
- `make install` - Download Go dependencies (`go mod download`)
- `make test` / `make test-all` - Run unit tests
- `make fmt` - Format code (Go, protobuf, shell)
- `make lint` - Lint Go code
- `make buf-gen` - Generate Protocol Buffer Go libraries and Swagger definitions
- `make clients` - Generate REST clients from Protocol Buffer definitions
- `make build-all-binaries` / `make build-changed-binaries` - Compile Go services
- `make check` - Run formatters, linting, and all tests (CI validation)

**Local Dependencies Stack (docker-compose.yaml):**
- **Kafka + Zookeeper** (ports 9092, 2181) - Message broker with auto-topic creation
- **Kafka UI** (port 9000) - kafdrop for Kafka visualization
- **MongoDB** (port 27017) - Database
- **Mongo Express** (port 9090) - MongoDB UI
- **Jaeger** (ports 6831-6832 UDP, 16686 HTTP) - Distributed tracing

**No Additional Local Dev Tools:**
- No Skaffold, Tilt, or Telepresence configuration found
- No .env.example or environment configuration files
- Monorepo uses standard Go project structure with services under `/services`

**Documentation:**
- Root README.md contains Makefile reference and contributing guidelines
- Commit message template enforces tagged format (`[feat]`, `[fix]`, etc.)
- Individual service READMEs focus on architecture/domain rather than setup

**Key Files:**
- /Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/Makefile (root entry point)
- /Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/.core/Makefile (shared tooling)
- /Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/docker/docker-compose.yaml (local services)
